pullRequest.title: Fix AttributeError when running lemur CLI without config
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

- Fix `getattr(script_info, 'config')` in `factory.py` to use a default value of `None`, preventing `AttributeError` when running `lemur --help` or `lemur` without a subcommand

When `create_app` is called during `--help` processing, the `script_info` object may not yet have the `config` attribute set (it's set by the `cli` callback in `manage.py`). Without a default, `getattr` raises `AttributeError`.

This addresses the first problem reported in #4764. The other problems mentioned (missing `@with_appcontext` on custom plugin CLI commands, and missing `@cli.command()` on `rotate`) appear to have been resolved in subsequent commits — `rotate` is correctly registered and visible in `lemur certificate --help`.

Closes #4764

## Test plan

- [x] `lemur --help` runs successfully and shows all commands
- [x] `lemur certificate --help` shows `rotate` and all other subcommands
- [x] Confirmed the original code raises `AttributeError` when `script_info` lacks the `config` attribute

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

pullRequest.title: Pass endpoint context to rotation notifications
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

- Adds an optional `endpoint` parameter to `send_rotation_notification()` in `messaging.py`
- Threads the endpoint through to `send_default_notification()` via `**kwargs`, making it available to notification plugins
- Updates the call site in `cli.py` to pass the endpoint being rotated

This is fully backwards compatible — the `endpoint` parameter defaults to `None`, and existing notification plugins already accept `**kwargs` in their `send()` signature.

Closes #5346

## Test plan

- [ ] Existing tests pass unchanged (they call `send_rotation_notification(cert)` without endpoint)
- [ ] Notification plugins that accept `**kwargs` can now access `endpoint` from kwargs when handling rotation events

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

pullRequest.title: Remove old certificate from endpoint after rotation
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

- Adds `remove_old_certificate(endpoint, old_cert)` to the `SourcePlugin` base class with a default no-op implementation
- Updates `deployment.service.rotate_certificate()` to call `remove_old_certificate()` after the new cert is attached
- Cleanup is best-effort: failures are logged as warnings but do not fail the rotation

This prevents old certificates from accumulating on endpoints that support multiple certificates (e.g. GCP load balancers), where stale certs cause `sync_all_sources` to create duplicate endpoint entries that grow linearly with each rotation cycle.

The no-op default is correct for providers like AWS ELBs that handle cert swaps atomically. Plugins for multi-cert endpoints (e.g. GCP) can override `remove_old_certificate()`.

Closes #5348

## Test plan

- [ ] Existing `test_rotate_certificate` passes (test source plugin inherits the no-op default)
- [ ] Existing email plugin rotation tests pass unchanged
- [ ] Source plugins that override `remove_old_certificate()` will have old certs cleaned up after rotation

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
